> Write an ADR for any decision that is **hard to reverse** — namespace, package type, client
> isolation model, data model, credential standard, framework choice ([Governance §6.2](../GOVERNANCE_MODEL.md)).
> Copy to `docs/decisions/ADR-NNN-{slug}.md`, using the next free number. Adding an ADR is 🟡 YELLOW
> (additive doc); implementing what it decides may carry its own 🔴 gate.

---

## Context

_The forces at play: what problem, what constraints, what makes this hard to reverse. State
production/runtime facts with confidence labels (`[Verified from source]` / `[Unverified]`).
One live production org (`00Dbn00000plgUfEAI`), small team, EDWOSB → ISV/public-company trajectory —
note which of these bear on the decision._

## Decision

_The decision, stated in the active voice ("Adopt …", "Use …"). Be specific enough that a reader
can act on it without guessing._

## Consequences

- **Positive:** _what this makes better / safer / cheaper._
- **Negative:** _the cost, ceremony, or risk accepted._
- **Follow-ups:** _any work this creates (docs to write, controls to add, migrations)._
